zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Mary Vincent於1506年出生在英國素瑞金斯頓 武朋 坦斯時，她的父親David Vincent Aylward28歲，母親Elizabeth Spencer Hodnot29歲。她於1526年與Sir John Evelyn在英國素瑞金斯頓結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1564年5月12日在英國英格蘭倫敦享年58歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Mary Vincent于1506年出生在英国素瑞金斯頓 武朋 坦斯时，她的父亲David Vincent Aylward28岁，母亲Elizabeth Spencer Hodnot29岁。她于1526年与Sir John Evelyn在英国素瑞金斯頓结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1564年5月12日在英国英格兰倫敦享年58岁。

nameMeanings.name: Vincent
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, French, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and Spanish: from the personal name Vincent, Latin Vincentius, a derivative of vincens, genitive vincentis, present participle of vincere ‘to conquer’. The name was borne by a 3rd-century Spanish martyr widely venerated in the Middle Ages and by a 5th-century monk and writer of Lérins, as well as various other early Christian saints.

Irish: the English surname (see 1 above) has been established in the south of Ireland since the 17th century, and has also been adopted as an English equivalent of Gaelic Mac Dhuibhinse ‘son of the dark man of the island’.

History: The surname Vincent of French origin (see 1 above) is listed in the register of Huguenot ancestors recognized by the Huguenot Society of America and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

variants.name: St. Vincent
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Leandre, Monique.

French (Saint-Vincent): habitational name from any of several places in various parts of France named Saint-Vincent (from the dedication of their churches to Saint Vincent), or a nickname with the prefix Saint as an (ironic) attachment to the personal name or surname Vincent .
History: Vincent Chamereau dit Saint-Vincent from Paris, France, married Marie-Thérèse Auban in Pierrefonds, QC, in 1752.

variants.name: Venson
meanings: English: altered form of   Vincent  .
French (Lot-et-Garonne): probably from a vernacular diminutive of the personal name   Vincent  . This surname is very rare in France.

variants.name: Vince
meanings: English (East Anglia): from a short form of the personal name   Vincent  .
Hungarian: variant of   Vincze  .

variants.name: Vicencio
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Anamaria, Javier, Jesus, Jorge, Juan, Manuel, Mario, Adriana, Alfonso, Amado, Baudelio.
Hispanic (mainly Mexico, Chile, and the Philippines); Portuguese (Vicêncio): from a vernacular form of the Latin personal name Vincentius (see Vincent ), a variant of Vicente .

variants.name: Vincze
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Arpad, Laszlo, Aranka, Attila, Bela, Ildiko, Kalman, Lajos, Mihaly, Miklos, Sandor, Zsolt.  Jewish Aron.
Hungarian: from the personal name Vince, Hungarian form of Vincent .
Jewish (from Hungary): adoption of the Hungarian surname in 1 above because it has some sounds in common with the original German-based surname.

variants.name: Sensel
meanings: South German: probably from a pet form of the Latin personal name Vincens (see Vincent ).
German: habitational name from any of the places called Senseln in former East Prussia and Bohemia.

variants.name: Vicente
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Armando, Carlos, Juan, Antonio, Sergio, Enrique, Jorge, Luis, Mario, Adriano, Esteban.  Portuguese Joaquim, Paulo, Aderito, Anabela, Joao, Martinho, Vasco.
Spanish and Portuguese: from the personal name Vicente, an equivalent of Vincent .

variants.name: Vincelette
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Aime, Alphonse, Fernand, Normand.
Americanized form of French Vincelet: derivative of Vincent . It reflects the Canadian and American French practice of sounding the final -t.

variants.name: Vincenti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Aldo, Angelo, Attilio, Carlo, Dante, Gino, Marco, Querino, Vito.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the personal name Vincente (see Vincent ). This surname is also found in Corsica and southern France.

variants.name: Vincenzo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Silvio, Angelo, Emilio, Fortunato, Mario.
Italian: from the personal name Vincenzo, a cognate ofVincente (see Vincent ).

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old French Marie. See Mary .

variants.name: Moira
meanings: Anglicized form of Irish Máire , now used throughout the English-speaking world.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Mary Vincent was born in 1506, in Kingston upon Thames, Surrey, England, her father, David Vincent Aylward, was 28 and her mother, Elizabeth Spencer Hodnot, was 29. She married Sir John Evelyn in 1526, in Kingston, Surrey, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died on 12 May 1564, in London, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 58.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Mary Vincent est née en 1506, à Kingston upon Thames, Surrey, Angleterre, son père, David Vincent Aylward, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Spencer Hodnot, avait 29 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John Evelyn en 1526, à Kingston, Surrey, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 12 mai 1564, à Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 58 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Mary Vincent 1506 in Kingston upon Thames, Surrey, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater David Vincent Aylward 28 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth Spencer Hodnot 29. Sie hat 1526 in Kingston, Surrey, England Sir John Evelyn geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 12. Mai 1564 im Alter von 58 Jahren in London, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Mary Vincent è nata il nel 1506 nel Kingston upon Thames, Surrey, England, sua padre, David Vincent Aylward, aveva 28 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth Spencer Hodnot, aveva 29 anni. Lei sposato Sir John Evelyn nel 1526, nel Kingston, Surrey, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 12 maggio 1564, nel London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 58 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Mary Vincent(이)가 1506년 영국 수리 킨그스턴 업 섬스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 David Vincent Aylward(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Spencer Hodnot(은)는 29세였다. 그녀는 1526년 영국 수리 킨그스턴에서 Sir John Evelyn(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1564년 5월 12일 영국 잉글랜드 런던에서 58세의 나이로 사망했다.
